stories.headline: Trump says Iran ceasefire is on 'massive life support'
stories.summary: The US president criticises an Iranian counteroffer to end the war, and says the month-long ceasefire is "unbelievably weak".

stories.headline: Trump skirts question on US ‘red lines’ for Iran ceasefire

stories.headline: Trump Says Americans’ Economic Pain Is Not a Consideration for the Iran War
stories.summary: Asked whether Americans’ financial situation was motivating him to make a deal, President Trump said bluntly, “Not even a little bit.”

stories.headline: EU needs to delay social media access for children - von der Leyen

stories.headline: BJP leader back to head India's Assam state for second time in a row

stories.headline: Macro Outlook “Much More Uncertain”, Says Aus. Treasurer
stories.summary: The macroeconomic outlook is “much more uncertain” with oil prices expected to remain elevated for some time yet and a slowdown in global growth, says Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers in budget speech. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Australia Tackles Property Tax Breaks in Budget
stories.summary: Australia will overhaul tax settings that have long favored property investors and assist households and firms grappling with soaring fuel costs in a budget presented by Treasurer Jim Chalmers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Hedge Fund CFM Opens Office in Shanghai, Assets Top $27 Billion

stories.headline: TIAA's Kourtney Gibson: Us Economy Showing Some ‘Wear'
stories.summary: TIAA CEO, Retirement Solutions Kourtney Gibson discusses retirement in a complex market landscape. She speaks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on "The Close."  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: AI Chipmaker Cerebras to Guide IPO Pricing Above Range
stories.summary: Cerebras Systems Inc. is guiding prospective investors that it expects to price its initial public offering above the top of its marketed range, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand soars for the chipmaker’s stock.

stories.headline: Mexico Rating Outlook Revised to Negative at S&P as Debts Mount
stories.summary: S&P Global Ratings revised Mexico’s credit outlook to negative from stable, citing persistently weak fiscal results, rising debt levels and weak economic growth.

stories.headline: Brazil Meat Left Out of EU Suppliers List in Risk to Trade
stories.summary: Brazil is absent from a list of countries authorized to supply animal products to the European Union, presenting a trade risk to the world’s largest beef and chicken exporter.

stories.headline: Japan’s Sojitz Eyes Southeast Asia for New Rare Earths Supply
stories.summary: Japanese trading house Sojitz Corp. is looking to Southeast Asia and other regions as new potential sources of rare earths outside Australia, as it aims to boost output and diversify its supply chain of the highly sought-after materials.

stories.headline: Jason Collins, First Active N.B.A. Player to Come Out as Gay, Dies at 47
stories.summary: His achievements on the court were eclipsed by an essay he wrote in Sports Illustrated in 2013 in which he declared: “I’m a 34-year-old N.B.A. center. I’m Black and I’m gay.”

stories.headline: Trump’s approach on China has changed, now appears focused on getting along
stories.summary: In his first term, the president demanded fundamental shifts in China’s economic model. Now, he has copied parts of how Beijing does business.
